page.content: The first table presents various structures associated with α-syn pathology, structural imaging, functional imaging, and molecular imaging. The Vagus nerve shows a positive α-syn pathology with NA for structural, functional, and molecular imaging.

molecular imaging. The Vagus nerve shows a positive α-syn pathology with NA for structural, functional, and molecular

cells.cell_text: Decreased 11C-donepezil standard uptake values in the small intestine and pancreas following a rostral-caudal gradient [43]
Bilateral reduction of olfactory bulb volume [64, 65, 68]
Decrease of olfactory sulcus depth; decrease of piriform cortex volume [68, 71]

Reduced activation in amygdala and hippocampal formation after olfactory stimulation [72–74]; decreased regional homogeneity and functional connectivity within olfactory cortex and decreased connectivity within limbic/paralimbic networks [75]

Reduced glucose metabolism in bilateral medial prefrontal cortex and parieto-occipito-temporal cortex [76]; positive correlations between UPSIT scores and acetylcholinesterase activities in hippocampal formation, amygdala and neocortex [70]; positive correlations between UPSIT scores and vesicular monoamine transporter type 2 binding potential in striatum [70]; positive correlations between UPSIT scores and dopamine transporter binding potential in hippocampus, amygdala and striatum [78]

page.content:
The Gastrointestinal tract indicates controversy in α-syn pathology, with NA for structural and functional imaging, and decreased 11C-donepezil standard uptake values in the small intestine and pancreas following a rostral-caudal gradient for molecular imaging. The Olfactory bulb has a positive α-syn pathology characterized by bilateral reduction of olfactory bulb volume in structural imaging, with NA for functional and molecular imaging. The Olfactory tract also shows positive α-syn pathology, with bilateral increase of mean diffusivity in structural imaging and NA for the other imaging types. The Olfactory cortex reveals positive α-syn pathology, a decrease of olfactory sulcus depth, and decrease of piriform cortex volume in structural imaging, reduced activation in the amygdala and hippocampal formation after olfactory stimulation in functional imaging, and reduced glucose metabolism in bilateral medial prefrontal cortex and parieto-occipito-temporal cortex in molecular imaging, along with positive correlations between UPSIT scores and various activities across multiple brain regions.

The second table compares PE (n = 3) and normal controls (n = 6) across maternal age, gestational age, SBP, DBP, proteinuria, infant birth weight, and placental weight. Maternal age is 31.2[2.16] in PE and 36.33[5.28] in controls. Gestational age is 36.09[0.95] in PE and 36.38[1.56] in controls. SBP is 173.25[31]*** in PE and 116.25[15.31] in controls. DBP is 109.5[13.2]*** in PE and 76.5[5.58] in controls.
